entry.title: Unlocking Data Potential: What I Learned from SAP Sapphire
entry.content:
Unlocking Data Potential: What I Learned from SAP Sapphire

Hi everyone! I’m Kara Reed, Senior Director of Product Marketing at SAP. At this year’s SAP Sapphire, I had the privilege of co-presenting a session with Brian Suk from Databricks on a topic that’s very close to my heart: how organizations can unlock the full value of their data through the combined power of SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks.

Our session—" SAP and Databricks open a bold new era of data and AI"—highlighted how businesses can build trusted, AI-powered applications with SAP Databricks, a fully SAP-managed solution that combines industry leading AI/ML, data science, and data engineering capabilities with semantically rich, business-ready data from SAP applications. Let me take you behind the scenes and share a few insights from the session—and what I took away from the event overall.

Driving Value with Data + AI

Our goal was to help attendees answer three key questions:

  • Why SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks?
  • What value can this bring to my organization?
  • How do they work together in practice?

It’s no secret that data and AI are reshaping the future of business. Companies that can turn their data into actionable insights—quickly, securely, and at scale—are the ones that will lead in their industries. But too many are still held back by siloed data, inconsistent quality, and tools that don’t talk to each other.

The Trust Gap is Real

One of the most striking stats we shared was from a recent survey of 1,200 business and IT leaders: only 34% have high trust in their data capabilities. That’s a wake-up call. Organizations are investing heavily in data platforms, yet many still struggle with aligning IT and business needs or ensuring data is ready for AI-driven use cases.

What SAP and Databricks Are Doing Differently

What stood out most in our session was showing how SAP and Databricks are partnering to change that. This is more than just a technical integration—it’s a strategic shift.

With SAP Business Data Cloud, customers can:

  • Access live, trusted SAP data products without duplication
  • Preserve business semantics and lineage
  • ML/AI development in pro-code environment

SAP Databricks in Business Data Cloud enables a zero-copy architecture that reduces time, effort, and risk when building production-grade AI solutions.

What Customers Told Me

One of my favorite parts of SAP Sapphire was speaking with customers one-on-one. Many were excited about how SAP-managed data products could drastically reduce the time and complexity of their data engineering tasks. The idea of getting "AI-ready" data out of SAP systems, without writing hundreds of custom pipelines, really resonated.

Several clients mentioned how this approach could help close the gap between IT and business teams, giving analysts and data scientists quicker access to meaningful, governed data. That’s a huge win for productivity and innovation.

My Takeaway

SAP Sapphire reminded me that we’re at a tipping point: data and AI are no longer "nice to have"—they’re critical to staying competitive. But what makes the difference isn’t just technology. It’s trust. Trust in the data. Trust in the platform. And trust that the tools will work together.

SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks are helping organizations make that leap.

Let’s Keep the Conversation Going

One of the best parts of the event? Meeting new people—partners, customers, colleagues—and hearing how you're tackling data and AI challenges in the real world. Let’s not stop here.

👉 Introduce yourself in the comments! I’d love to hear what you're working on and how you see data + AI evolving at your organization.

And if you're looking to go deeper, here are some helpful resources:

  • Try it out!
  • Business Data Cloud help documentation

Let’s keep learning from each other.

Related community managed tags you should subscribe to remain informed about this topic:   

 Data and Analytics SAP Datasphere SAP Business Data Cloud

    

Related community topic pages:   

Data and Analytics  SAP Business Data Cloud  SAP Datasphere

entry.title: How to Send Automated GR Email Notifications to Buyers in SAP Using Output Determination
entry.content:
In many organisations, the buyer or procurement team must be notified when a goods receipt (GR) is posted for a Purchase Order (PO). This ensures prompt actions such as invoice verification, quality checks, or inventory updates. SAP provides a standard functionality that uses output determination to automate notifications via system-generated messages.

In this blog, we will walk through the steps to configure an automatic GR message notification to the buyer's inbox (SAP Business Workplace) when a goods receipt is posted in a specific plant and storage location.

🏢 Business Scenario

Imagine a manufacturing company that receives raw materials at multiple plants. The procurement team responsible for Plant 1000 and Storage Location 1004 wants to be alerted as soon as goods are received at this location so they can initiate inspection and payment processes quickly.

To automate this, we can configure a GR Message output type (MLGR) that triggers a system mail to the buyer whenever a GR is posted at that specific plant/storage location.

Step 1: Create Condition Table

Path: SPRO → Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Output Determination → Maintain Condition Tables → Output: Create Conditions Table

Create a new condition table (e.g., 914) that includes key fields such as Plant and Storage Location.

Step 2: Maintain Access Sequence

Path: SPRO → Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Output Determination → Maintain Access Sequences

Assign the newly created condition table (914) to access sequence 0002 (used for GR emails).

Step 3: Maintain Output Type

Path: SPRO → Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Output Determination → Maintain Output Types

 

  • Use or customise the MLGR output type.

  • Navigate to Mail title and Texts to define the subject and message body for the GR message.

Navigate to mail title and texts

Step 4: Maintain Output Determination Procedure

Path: SPRO → Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Output Determination → Maintain Output Determination Procedures

Ensure Requirement 171 is assigned (this requirement is specific to GR email functionality)

Step 5: Maintain Condition Record (Transaction: MN21)

 

  • Enter the condition type MLGR.

  • Provide plant and storage location values.

  • On the Communication tab, enter the SAP user ID of the buyer.

  • Select Express Mail if instant pop-up notification is required.

Note: You may get a pop-up assigning a dummy recipient—click Yes to proceed, when clicked on Communication.

Step 6: Create Purchase Order (Transaction: ME21N)

 

  • In the PO Header, navigate to the Delivery/Invoice tab.

  • Ensure the checkbox GR Message is selected.

Step 7: Perform Goods Receipt (Transaction: MIGO)

 

  • Post the GR using MIGO for the PO.

  • Make sure the storage location matches the one configured in the condition record.

Step 8: Check Buyer Inbox (Transaction: SBWP)

 

  • Go to SBWP (SAP Business Workplace).

  • The buyer should see a new message containing the GR details.

Since Note was maintained in step 5, it will appear here. 

Step 9: Option: Review via SOST (Mail Log)

 

  • Execute transaction SOST to check the email/message log.

  • Click on Choose to view the actual message content sent to the buyer.

If you found this guide helpful, don’t miss my other SAP MM blogs covering real-world automation, configuration tips, and best practices for procurement and inventory management.

Click to view other SAP MM Blogs - SAP Community

 

Regards,

Leelamohan Mannem

entry.title: How to create a Dropdown List Variable to prefilter an SAPB1 User Query?
entry.content:
Hi all. I'm trying to figure out if it's possible to create a user query that uses a predefined list of options/values, which dependent on the option picked, will filter the query data on output.

To give a basic example, the OITM table and its ItemCode and OnHand fields. On execution, I want the Selection Criteria to provide a single input, being a dropdown list of the strings "ALL", "POS", "NEG", and "ZERO". Depending on which option is picked by the user, the returning query data would be the ItemCode and OnHand columns for items where the OnHand value is > 0, < 0, or = 0 (with "ALL" applying no filtering to the data).

I know that this can be done within the query preview using the filter table option, but I want to implement a preset filtering in the query from the start, as to potentially reduce the number of returning rows so the query doesn't take as long to generate.

This here was my initial attempt based on the resources I could find about this topic, but it didn't work (and I feel like the '/Dropdown/' syntax isn't correct here either):

SELECT T0.ItemCode, T0.OnHand
FROM OITM T0
WHERE
([%0/Dropdown/ALL,POS,NEG,ZERO] = 'ALL')
OR ([%0] = 'POS' AND T0.OnHand > 0)
OR ([%0] = 'NEG' AND T0.OnHand < 0)
OR ([%0] = 'ZERO' AND T0.OnHand = 0)

Any help here would be highly appreciative. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Add Custom Fields in S/4HANA Public Cloud Migration C...
entry.content:
Hi,

Document and Document Version are document info record fields. To add a document info record to a product, you must use the related data migration object for document info records: 

Document info record


https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/e0c077f8cd8c413abedacec4f71fc61f.html 

Inspection Text, Basic Data Text, Internal Comment are long text. To migrate Product long text you must use: 

Product - extend existing record with long text

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/6a317abf80aa4e7598b690bc394688d5.html 

Text Object Text ID

MATERIALBEST (Purchasing text)
MATERIALGRUN (Basic text)
MATERIALIVER (Internal note)
MATERIALPRUE (Inspection text)
MDTXTLTXT (MRP text)
MVKE0001 (Sales text)

In Public Cloud you can only use the Modeler App to add fields that are enabled to be enhanced by SAP.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict access to view of supplier invoices i...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If you’re working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and want to stop users (like people on B2B contracts) from seeing each other’s supplier invoices in the Supplier Invoice List app even though they’re in the same Company Code and Purchasing Group  the key is to control access based on the Cost Center.

Right now, since they share the same company and purchasing group, they’ll normally be able to see all supplier invoices. But if each person is only responsible for their own Cost Center, we can use that as the restriction point.

 

Here’s how you can do it:

1. Create a custom business role
Go to the app called “Maintain Business Roles”
Find the role your users are using for example, SAP_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT and copy it to create a custom one, like ZBR_AP_LIMITED.

 

2. Set up access restrictions
In the new custom role, go to the “Maintain Restrictions” tab.
Look for a restriction type called C_ACCTASGN – Account Assignment.
This lets you limit access based on things like Cost Center, Internal Order, etc.

    • Choose “Restricted”
    • Add the Cost Center(s) the user should be allowed to see
    • Leave all other cost centers out

This will make sure each person only sees invoices related to their own cost center not someone else’s.

  • Assign the custom role to each user
    Use the Maintain Business Users app to assign the right role to each person, with the matching cost center restriction.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: DDIC Structure for Migration Cockpit Objects
entry.content:
Hello Viktorvk,

the sender structures S_* are no DDIC structures, they only exists as  entries within the migration workbench configuration tables.

The structures are then generated into the coding. But each sender structure of a migration project is available as temporary table on the SAP HANA database: 
If you are an on-premises or Private Cloud customer you will see that tables within SE11 per migration project. You need to have the three letter Mass transfer ID of your project. The naming convention of a structure is as follows:

see 2733253 - FAQ for SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit. Migration approach: Transfer / Migrate data using staging tables - SAP for Me

  • Below is an example for a generated staging table for object Work center (SIF_WORK_CNTR) and structure S_KAZY

  • Table name: /1LT/DS<sys-id><consecutive number>

    • For example: /1LT/DSQKX000928

  • Table description: Z_<Alias = technical Object name without SIF_>_<Mass transfer ID>:<structure name>

    • For example: Z_WORK_CNTR_U02:S_KAZY

Best is to search for then name via description.

The last two fields are not part of the sender structure, they are status flags for the data migration. Also the first field MANDT is not part of the defined sender structure

BR
Frank

Hello!

Without knowing your configuratiuon and setup, that type of error could be pointing to a couple of different things that you can check:

Data mapping to populate the your Coordinator role (T). Field might be missing from the template entirely, or it's not correctly mapped to a corresponding value or field in the position data. Might need to check the position to requisition mapping rule as well.

Check the requisition template:

  • to examine the field mappings to see if it's correctly linked to the appropriate position data or employee data fields.If the
  • If the template is outdated or there were some recent changes and try updating it to the latest version in an attempt to repair it.

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Data Migration Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Karthick

nice blog, one comment to your statement:

"The uploaded template file must be less than 100 MB in size. If your data is too large, consider splitting it into multiple files."

You can also use ZIP files. To avoid splitting in multiple files you can also try to use CSV files instead of the XML files. Using CSV files, you can easily tailor your files and only need to use the files for the structures and fields you want to process. A CSV file has much less administrative data than an XML file. The 100 MB limit is the same, but with tailored CSV files this http-protocol related size limit is not reached that fast as using XML files.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Optional/Required field change via Migration Object M...
entry.content:
Hello Naresha,

that message ID MM 85 is thrown by the API used to migrate the data. That error message has nothing to do with the visibility definition of the migration template. Within the migration template you will only see fields being marked as mandatory that are always mandatory- Fields that can be maintained mandatory or not mandatory via business process configuration or fields that are dependent on field value combinations mandatory or not mandatory are never marked as mandatory within the migration template. Each sheet/structure of the migration template has an assigned database table. Setting a field Mandatory or not is done via database constraints. Changing database constraints dynamically is not possible. 

For the API the material group is a mandatory field. You must either verify if you can configure this field within the different business process settings for material master to be not mandatory or you must provide a value for that field. 
You can also check the KBA 2756835 - Material Group MARA-MATKL is mandatory for MM02 despite set as optional - SAP for Me
There it is explained that if data for purchasing view is being used, material group is always mandatory.

BR
Frank

How to create a ticket with the Job Name in the subject?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Subject of Service now ticket created from Cloud ALM...
entry.content:
Hello, 

The built-in mapping for Service now does not include the job name in the subject of the ticket.
In order to include the required fields kindly review and adjust the mapping as per instructed in "Create Mapping" section of Integration with External Ticket Systems.
The available fields for usage are described in https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/event-situations

In order to include the job name in the subject you may include one of the following fields:
-tags.subject -> Event name + Job name
-tags.NAME -> Job name

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Custom field and logics
entry.content:
Hello Fatima,

have you checked the customer migration object's documentation (custom field section)?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/b251af240d9c41e9b9baf94603b9491a.html

the business context is assigned 

 

Business Context Structure

Master Data: Business Partner (BP_CUSTVEND1)General Data (S_CUST_GEN)
Customer Core View (CUSTOMER_GENERAL)General Data (S_CUST_GEN)
Customer company code core (CUST_COMPANYCODE)Company Data (S_CUST_COMPANY)
Partner Sales area core view (CUST_SALES)Sales Data (S_CUST_SALES_DATA)

Each business context is assigned to an API structure and that API structure is assigned to a sender structure within the migration object. There is no API structure existing for Key-user-extensibility support for tax data.
The business context Customerer Core View (Customer General) is assigned to S_CUST_GEN (General Data) structure. Fields within that business context are only visible in that structure. They cannot be made visible in another structure than the one mentioned within the documentation.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: data action to copy data by version and date from cla...
entry.content:
@Raj4 

Its difficult to provide a working code without model knowledge. But LINK syntax is something like below

DATA() = LINK([AccountModel], [d/Version] = "Plan01",  [d/Entity] = "REG0001",[d/Product] = "PRD0002", [d/Customer] = "CUST0001")

You can refer to the syntax here

Change Values in Advanced Formulas with DATA, RESULTLOOKUP, and LINK | SAP Help Portal 

 

Hope this helps

Nikhil
